input.content:
# Phantom Shell User Guide

**Browser-Native Development Environment**

---

## What is Phantom Shell?

Phantom Shell is a **browser-native terminal** that runs directly in your browser. It provides a real development environment without installing anything on your computer.

**Key Features:**
- Full terminal emulator (not a chat interface)
- Runs Node.js and npm commands in your browser via WebContainers
- Command history with up/down arrow navigation
- Project persistence (saved to IndexedDB)
- Works entirely offline after initial load
- Zero data sent to servers - privacy-first

---

## Accessing Phantom Shell

1. Click the **AI Terminal** button (💬) in the header or press `Ctrl+Shift+A` / `Cmd+Shift+A`
2. You'll see two tabs at the top: **💬 Chat** and **⌨️ Shell**
3. Click **⌨️ Shell** to switch to Phantom Shell mode

---

## Built-in Commands

Phantom Shell supports several built-in commands (prefix with `/`):

### Help & Info

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/help` | Show all available commands |
| `/version` | Show version info and WebContainer status |
| `/about` | About Phantom Shell |
| `/clear` | Clear the terminal screen |
| `/history` | Show your recent command history |

### Project Management

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/projects` | List all your projects |
| `/new <name>` | Create a new project |
| `/open <name>` | Open an existing project |
| `/close` | Close the current project |
| `/export` | Export project to .brain file (coming soon) |

### AI Commands (Coming Soon)

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/ai <prompt>` | Ask AI to help with development |

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Enter` | Execute command |
| `↑` / `↓` | Navigate command history |
| `Ctrl+C` | Cancel current input |
| `Backspace` | Delete last character |

---

## System Commands (WebContainer)

Once WebContainer boots, you can run standard Node.js commands:

```bash
# Check Node.js version
node -v

# Initialize a project
npm init -y

# Install packages
npm install lodash

# Run Node.js scripts
node index.js

# Run npm scripts
npm run dev
```

**Note:** WebContainer requires Cross-Origin Isolation headers. You'll see a ✓ or ✗ status in `/version` output.

---

## Browser Requirements

Phantom Shell uses WebContainers which requires modern browser features:

| Browser | Support |
|---------|---------|
| Chrome 89+ | ✅ Full support |
| Edge 89+ | ✅ Full support |
| Firefox 89+ | ✅ Full support |
| Safari 16.4+ | ✅ Full support |

**Technical Requirements:**
- SharedArrayBuffer support
- Cross-Origin Isolation (COEP/COOP headers)

---

## Project Templates

When creating a new project with `/new`, you can use templates:

### React + Vite (`react-vite`)
- React 18 with Vite bundler
- Hot module replacement (HMR)
- Ready to run `npm run dev`

### Node.js Basic (`node-basic`)
- Minimal Node.js project
- ES Modules enabled
- Ready for server-side development

---

## Data Storage

All your Phantom Shell data is stored locally:

- **Projects:** Saved to IndexedDB (persists across sessions)
- **Command History:** Saved locally (last 1000 commands)
- **Files:** Stored in browser memory during session, persisted to IndexedDB

**Privacy:** No data is sent to any server. Everything runs locally in your browser.

---

## Troubleshooting

### WebContainer Not Available

If you see "WebContainer requires Cross-Origin Isolation":

1. Make sure you're using a supported browser
2. Check that you're accessing via `https://` or `localhost`
3. Verify headers are configured (for self-hosted deployments)

### Commands Not Executing

1. Wait for WebContainer to finish booting (2-5 seconds)
2. Check `/version` for WebContainer status
3. Try `/help` to verify built-in commands work

### Terminal Not Responding

1. Try pressing `Ctrl+C` to cancel
2. Click the **💬 Chat** tab and back to **⌨️ Shell**
3. Refresh the page if needed (your projects are saved)

---

## Coming Soon

- **Phase 3:** IndexedDB persistence verification
- **Phase 4:** AI-assisted development (`/ai create react app`)
- **Phase 5:** Live preview pane for dev servers
- **Phase 6:** Git integration via isomorphic-git

---

## Technical Details

Phantom Shell is built with:

- **xterm.js** - Full-featured terminal emulator
- **WebContainers** - Node.js runtime in browser (WebAssembly)
- **Zustand** - State management with persistence
- **IndexedDB** - Local project storage

**Bundle Impact:** ~200KB (lazy loaded when you open the terminal)

---

**Related Docs:**
- [AI Terminal Guide](./ai_terminal_guide.md) - Chat with AI providers
- [Feature Map](./feature_map.md) - Full feature inventory

**Purpose:** Complete feature inventory and categorization
**Audience:** Product, engineering, design

🚧 **In Progress** - Currently being developed

## 15. Strategic Frameworks & Influences 💡

### 15.1 Rob Dyrdek's "Machine Mindset" & Existence Platform 💡 P2 - 2027
**Philosophy:** Design time, don't just manage it. Life is a system to optimize.

**Platform Reference:** Rob Dyrdek's **Existence** (launched November 2025)
- "Time intelligence platform" that implements the Machine Mindset philosophy
- Tags activities as Life/Work/Health/Sleep with time block tracking
- Uncovers "hidden patterns shaping your life" through qualitative tracking
- **Market Validation:** Major celebrity entrepreneur just launched exactly what we're building!

**Core Mechanics (from Machine Mindset/Existence):**
- **Time Blocking:** Every hour has a purpose (integrate with Calendar)
- **Zero-Based Time Budgeting:** Allocate every minute like a dollar
- **Qualitative Tracking:** Daily 0-10 ratings for Life, Work, Health (5+ years of data)
- **Life Rhythms:** Weekly/monthly/quarterly review cadence
- **"Rhythm of Existence" Document:** Guide covering time, health, money, family/work integration

**Implementation Ideas for Neuman Brain:**
- 💡 Daily rating prompts (Life/Work/Health 0-10) - inspired by Existence
- 💡 Qualitative trend visualization (line charts over time)
- 💡 Time block templates (morning routine, deep work, etc.)
- 💡 Weekly reflection prompts
- 💡 Pattern detection across Life/Work/Health categories

**Our Differentiator:** Local-first privacy (vs Existence's likely cloud-based approach)

### 15.2 PMP & ITIL for Personal Life 💡 P2 - 2027
**Philosophy:** Enterprise-grade rigor applied to life management

**PMP (Project Management Professional) Application:**
- 💡 "Life Projects" module (major goals as projects)
  - Project Charter (scope, timeline, budget)
  - Stakeholders (who's involved in this life goal?)
  - Critical Path (dependencies between life tasks)
- 💡 Example: "Buy a house" project with tasks, milestones, deadlines

**ITIL (Service Management) Application:**
- 💡 Personal Service Level Agreements (SLAs)
  - "I agree to workout 3x/week" → track adherence
  - "Service Review" triggers on SLA breach
- 💡 Continuous Improvement loops
  - Retrospectives on failed habits
  - Root cause analysis for missed goals

- Launched mid-November 2025 (approximately 2 weeks ago)
- Celebrity entrepreneur (Ridiculousness host, pro skateboarder, business mogul)
- Dyrdek has tracked every second of his life for 5+ years

**Features (Known):**
- Time block tracking (Life/Work/Health/Sleep categories)
- Journaling/documentation of activities
- Pattern detection ("hidden patterns shaping your life")
- Calendar organization with activity tagging
- Qualitative tracking (0-10 daily ratings)

**Technology Stack:** Unknown (likely cloud-based SaaS)

**Pricing:** Unknown (likely subscription model)

**Our Strategic Position vs Existence:**

| Feature | Existence (Dyrdek) | Neuman Brain (Us) | Advantage |
|---------|-------------------|-------------------|-----------|
| **Privacy** | Likely cloud-based | **Local-first, zero-data-host** | **Us** (privacy-absolute) |
| **Data Ownership** | Their servers | **User's IndexedDB** | **Us** (sovereign data) |
| **Open Source** | Proprietary | **Could go open-source** | **Us** (transparency) |
| **AI Integration** | Unknown | **8 providers, BYOK, encrypted keys** | **Us** (flexibility) |
| **Time Tracking** | Time blocks | **71% Toggl parity (shipped)** | **Us** (professional features) |
| **Task Management** | Unknown | **98% Jira/Linear parity (shipped)** | **Us** (PM-grade) |
| **Notes/PKM** | Unknown | **50% Obsidian parity (shipped)** | **Us** (knowledge management) |
| **Calendar** | Basic organization | **65% Google Calendar parity (shipped)** | **Us** (full scheduling) |
| **Market Validation** | **Celebrity launch, media buzz** | Small indie project | **Them** (marketing) |
| **Development Stage** | **Live product (Nov 2025)** | MVP (147 features shipped) | **Them** (first-mover) |
| **Life OS Vision** | Time intelligence focus | **Five Spheres (holistic)** | **Us** (comprehensive) |

**Market Validation:**
- ✅ Existence launch proves market demand for "Life OS" platforms
- ✅ Celebrity endorsement validates time intelligence concept
- ✅ Timing is perfect: we're building the privacy-first alternative
- ⚠️ They have first-mover advantage and celebrity marketing
- ✅ We have superior feature set already (Kanban/Notes/Calendar vs their time blocks)

**Strategic Opportunities:**
1. **Privacy-First Alternative:** "Existence without the privacy invasion"
2. **Power User Features:** We have PM tools (Kanban, dependencies, subtasks) they likely don't
3. **Knowledge Management:** Our notes/PKM integration is unique
4. **AI Flexibility:** 8 providers vs their (likely) single AI integration
5. **Open Source Potential:** Could release as open-source to differentiate

- Track feature releases to stay competitive
- Study their UX/UI patterns for inspiration (not copying)
- Observe user feedback on their platform

| **Performance** | 5 | 2 | 0 | 7 |
| **AI & Automation** | 8 | 6 | 8 | 22 |
| **Dashboard Widgets** | 40+ | 3 | 2 | 45+ |
| **Life OS (Five Spheres)** | 0 | 0 | 50+ | 50+ |
| **TOTAL** | **147+** | **64** | **116+** | **327+** |

**Progress:** ~45% of planned features shipped (147/327), ~80% of MVP features complete

---

**Completion by Priority:**
- **P0 (Critical):** 80% complete - Notes, Calendar, Dashboard core features shipped
- **P1 (High):** 40% complete - Advanced features partially implemented
- **P2 (Medium):** 20% complete - Phase 4-6 features planned
- **P3 (Nice-to-have):** 5% complete - Future roadmap items

---

## Appendix: Feature Parity Methodology

### How Parity Percentages Are Calculated

**Objective:** Provide transparent, verifiable parity calculations to set accurate user expectations.

**Formula:**
```
Parity % = (Shipped Features / Competitor Baseline Features) × 100
```

**Competitor Baseline:** Count of essential features in the competitor's core product tier (not enterprise-only features).

---

### Detailed Parity Calculations

- **Neuman Brain Shipped:** 18 features

16. Nested lists (✅ Shipped)

**Why 50% is accurate:** We have solid markdown editing (Lexical), but missing the knowledge graph features (wiki-links, backlinks, graph view) that define Obsidian.

---

#### Notes: 50% Notion Parity

**Calculation:**

- **Parity:** 18/36 = 50%

---

#### Dashboard Widgets: 70% Notion Parity

**Calculation:**

- ...and 55+ more widget types

**Why 70% is accurate:** We have more widget variety (49 vs Notion's ~30), but missing drag-drop customization and widget templates.

---

#### Calendar: 65% Google Calendar Parity

**Calculation:**
- **Google Calendar Baseline:** 60 core features (enterprise excluded)
- **Neuman Brain Shipped:** 39 features
- **Parity:** 39/60 = 65%

**Missing features (21 total):**
- Recurring events (major feature)

---

#### Time Tracking: 71% Toggl Parity (Phase 1+2+3 Complete - MVP ✅)

**Calculation:**
- **Toggl Baseline:** 35 core features
- **Neuman Brain Shipped:** 25 features (Phase 1: 10, Phase 2: 8, Phase 3: 7)
- **Parity:** 25/35 = 71.4% ≈ 71%

**Shipped features (25 total):**
1. Start/stop timer (✅ Phase 1)
2. Pause/resume timer (✅ Phase 1)
3. Real-time timer display (✅ Phase 1)
4. Timer description input (✅ Phase 1)
5. Recent entries (48h history) (✅ Phase 1)
6. Today's total time (✅ Phase 1)
7. Continue from entry (✅ Phase 1)
8. Timer UI panel (Toggl-style) (✅ Phase 1)
9. Panel collapse/expand (✅ Phase 1)
10. Drag-to-resize panel (✅ Phase 1)
11. Time entry editing (✅ Phase 2)
12. Time entry deletion (✅ Phase 2)
13. Daily time summary (✅ Phase 2)
14. Weekly time summary (✅ Phase 2)
15. Detailed entry list (✅ Phase 2)
16. Date range filters (✅ Phase 2)
17. Pagination (✅ Phase 2)
18. Time formatting utilities (✅ Phase 1)
19. Manual time entry (✅ Phase 3)
20. Project support (create, assign, filter) (✅ Phase 3)
21. Monthly reports (✅ Phase 3)
22. Project breakdown reports (✅ Phase 3)
23. Export to CSV (✅ Phase 3)
24. Calendar view (✅ Phase 3)
25. Bulk operations (select, delete, update) (✅ Phase 3)

**Missing features (10 total):**
- Tags (❌ Planned Phase 4)
- Billable/non-billable toggle (❌ Planned Phase 4)
- Hourly rates (❌ Planned Phase 4)
- Invoice generation (❌ Future)
- Idle detection (❌ Future)
- Auto-tracking (❌ Future)
- Rounding rules (❌ Future)
- Team time tracking (❌ Future)
- Integrations (Toggl import) (❌ Future)
- Quick-start from Kanban task (❌ Planned Phase 4)

**Why 71% is accurate:** Phase 3 added all core professional features: manual entry, projects, monthly reports, CSV export, calendar view, and bulk operations. We now have feature parity with Toggl Free tier. Missing only advanced billing (hourly rates, invoicing) and team features.

---

#### AI Terminal: 60% ChatGPT Parity

**Calculation:**
- **ChatGPT Baseline:** 25 core features
- **Neuman Brain Shipped:** 15 features
- **Parity:** 15/25 = 60%

**Missing features (10 total):**
- Voice input
- Voice output
- Image generation (DALL-E)
- Web browsing mode
- Code interpreter
- File uploads
- Custom instructions
- Conversation folders
- Shared conversations
- ChatGPT plugins

**Why 60% is accurate:** We have multi-provider support (8 providers vs ChatGPT's 1), encryption, usage tracking, and auto-fallback. Missing advanced features like voice, images, and plugins.

---

### Verification Process

**How to verify these calculations:**

1. **Open competitor product** (Obsidian, Notion, Jira, etc.)
2. **List all core features** in basic tier (exclude enterprise/plugins)
3. **Count features** in each category
4. **Check Neuman Brain codebase:**
   - Search for implementations
   - Verify in UI (npm run dev)
   - Check task files for "Shipped" status
5. **Calculate:** (Shipped / Total) × 100

**Example for Kanban:**
```bash
# Search for subtask implementation
grep -r "subtask" src/widgets/Kanban/
# Found: SubtaskList.tsx, subtask creation logic, progress tracking

# Search for dependencies
grep -r "dependencies" src/widgets/Kanban/
# Found: dependencies array, blocker logic, visualization

# Search for ListView
ls src/widgets/Kanban/ListView.tsx
# Found: Complete ListView implementation with sorting, filtering, bulk ops
```

**Result:** 49/50 features verified in codebase = 98% parity ✅

---

### Transparency Note

**Why we document methodology:**

1. **Set accurate expectations** - Users know exactly what we have vs don't have
2. **No marketing fluff** - Every percentage is verifiable via codebase
3. **Track progress** - Easy to update as features ship
4. **Honest comparisons** - We don't inflate numbers or cherry-pick competitors
5. **Engineering rigor** - Treat documentation with same precision as code

**Philosophy:** If we claim 98% Kanban parity, you should be able to verify all 49 shipped features by reading the code. If we can't defend a number with evidence, we don't publish it.

---

**Related:**
[Product Roadmap](feature_roadmap.md) - Phased delivery plan
[User Gaps](../strategy/user_expectations_gaps.md) - Feature gaps
[Implementation Plan](../technical/implementation_plan.md) - Sprint breakdown
[CHANGELOG](../CHANGELOG.md) - Release history

**Document Status:** ✅ Active
**Last Updated:** November 25, 2025

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Phantom Shell UI Integration & Firefox Localhost Fix

**Date:** 2025-11-25
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** High
**Estimated Time:** 2-3 hours

---

## Overview

This task addresses two related issues and adds user documentation:

1. **Firefox Localhost Bug** - Buttons in AI Terminal don't respond on Firefox localhost (but work in production)
2. **Phantom Shell UI Integration** - Connect the Terminal component to the app so users can actually see and test it
3. **User Documentation** - Create user-facing docs for Phantom Shell features

**User Problem:**
- Cannot interact with AI Terminal on Firefox localhost
- Cannot see or test the Phantom Shell terminal (it exists but isn't rendered)
- No documentation explaining how to use Phantom Shell

**Technical Problem:**
- COEP/COOP headers may be causing button click issues in Firefox dev mode
- PhantomShell Terminal component exists but isn't integrated into any visible UI
- Missing user documentation for the new feature

---

## Problem Statement

### Issue A: Firefox Localhost Buttons
**Current behavior:** Clicking buttons in AI Terminal does nothing on Firefox localhost
**Desired behavior:** Buttons should work the same as in production
**User impact:** Developer cannot test locally in Firefox

### Issue B: Phantom Shell UI Integration
**Current behavior:** Terminal component exists in `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` but isn't rendered anywhere
**Desired behavior:** Users can access and use the Phantom Shell terminal
**User impact:** Cannot test or use the browser-native terminal feature

### Issue C: Missing Documentation
**Current behavior:** No user-facing documentation for Phantom Shell
**Desired behavior:** Clear docs explaining features and how to use them
**User impact:** Users won't know how to use the new feature

---

## Files to Read

- [x] `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` - Current AI Terminal implementation
- [x] `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` - Phantom Shell terminal component
- [x] `src/services/phantomShell/shellService.ts` - Command execution
- [x] `src/stores/usePhantomShellStore.ts` - State management
- [x] `vite.config.ts` - COEP/COOP header configuration
- [ ] `docs/product/feature_map.md` - Where to document new feature

---

## Proposed Changes

### Part A: Debug Firefox Localhost Issue

**Investigation Steps:**
1. Check browser console for COEP/COOP related errors
2. Test if `crossOriginIsolated` is true in Firefox
3. Check if button event handlers are being blocked
4. Try disabling COEP headers temporarily to isolate the issue

**Potential Fixes:**
- Add `credentialless` to COEP header for more permissive mode
- Check if Firefox needs different header configuration
- Investigate if service worker or caching is involved

### Part B: Phantom Shell UI Integration

**Option 1: Add to AITerminal Sidebar (Recommended)**
- Add a toggle/tab to switch between AI Chat and Phantom Shell terminal
- Reuses existing sidebar infrastructure
- Keeps terminal accessible from main dashboard

**Option 2: Dedicated Route/Page**
- Create new `/phantom-shell` route
- Full-screen terminal experience
- More complex but gives more space

**Recommendation:** Option 1 - integrate into existing AITerminal sidebar with a mode toggle

**Files to Modify:**
1. `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` - Add mode toggle and Terminal integration
2. `src/components/Header.tsx` - Possibly add Phantom Shell indicator/button

### Part C: User Documentation

**Files to Create:**
1. `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` - User guide for Phantom Shell
2. Update `docs/product/feature_map.md` - Add Phantom Shell to feature list

---

## Todo Checklist

### Phase A: Firefox Localhost Debug (30 min)
- [ ] Investigate Firefox console for COEP/COOP errors
- [ ] Test `crossOriginIsolated` status in Firefox
- [ ] Try alternative COEP header configuration
- [ ] Document findings and fix

### Phase B: UI Integration (1.5 hours)
- [ ] Add mode toggle to AITerminal (Chat vs Terminal)
- [ ] Integrate PhantomShell Terminal component
- [ ] Connect terminal to shell executor
- [ ] Add keyboard input handling
- [ ] Test basic commands (/help, /clear, /version)
- [ ] Test WebContainer boot and status display

### Phase C: Documentation (45 min)
- [ ] Create `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md`
- [ ] Update `docs/product/feature_map.md`
- [ ] Add inline help text to UI

### Phase D: Testing & Verification (30 min)
- [ ] Test Firefox localhost after fixes
- [ ] Test Chrome/Edge localhost
- [ ] Test production build
- [ ] Run type-check and lint
- [ ] Verify all 79 tests still pass

---

## Design Considerations

### UI Integration Design
- **Toggle UI:** Simple tab/button to switch between "AI Chat" and "Terminal" modes
- **State Preservation:** Keep chat messages when switching modes
- **Visual Indicator:** Show which mode is active (highlight/underline)

### Accessibility
- Terminal should be keyboard accessible
- Screen reader should announce mode changes
- Focus should move appropriately when switching modes

---

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Severity | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|------------|
| Firefox fix breaks other browsers | High | Low | Test all browsers after fix |
| COEP changes break WebContainer | High | Medium | Test WebContainer boot after changes |
| Terminal integration breaks AI chat | Medium | Low | Keep modes separate, test both |

---

## Session Log

### 2025-11-25 - Task Creation
- Created task file for Firefox fix + UI integration
- Ready for user approval before implementation

---

content.text:
Now let me provide a summary of what's been implemented and what you need to test:

---

## Implementation Complete!

**Committed:** `b0544dc` - feat(phantom-shell): Add UI integration and user documentation

### What Was Implemented:

1. **Mode Toggle in AI Terminal**
   - Two tabs at the top: **💬 Chat** and **⌨️ Shell**
   - Click to switch between AI chat and Phantom Shell

2. **Phantom Shell Terminal Integration**
   - Full xterm.js terminal with keyboard input
   - Command execution with real output
   - History navigation (up/down arrows)
   - Ctrl+C cancellation

3. **User Documentation**
   - Created `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` with full user guide
   - Updated feature map with Phantom Shell section

---

## How to Test Phantom Shell

### Step 1: Open AI Terminal
Press `Ctrl+Shift+A` (or `Cmd+Shift+A` on Mac) OR click the chat button in header

### Step 2: Switch to Shell Mode
Click the **⌨️ Shell** tab in the terminal header

### Step 3: Try These Commands

```bash
# Get help
/help

# Check version and WebContainer status
/version

# Clear screen
/clear

# View command history
/history

# Create a project
/new my-app

# List projects
/projects
```

### Step 4: Test WebContainer Commands (if supported)
```bash
# Check Node version
node -v

# Simple Node execution
node -e "console.log('Hello from browser!')"
```

---

## Firefox Localhost Issue

I still need your debugging feedback. Please run these in Firefox DevTools Console (F12):

```javascript
// 1. Check cross-origin isolation
console.log('crossOriginIsolated:', typeof crossOriginIsolated !== 'undefined' ? crossOriginIsolated : 'undefined');

// 2. Check if buttons exist and their state
document.querySelectorAll('button').forEach((btn, i) => {
  console.log(`Button ${i}:`, btn.textContent?.trim().slice(0,20), '- disabled:', btn.disabled);
});

// 3. Check for service workers
navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations().then(regs => console.log('Service workers:', regs.length));
```

**Please share:**
1. What do you see when clicking buttons? (Nothing? Cursor change?)
2. Any console errors when clicking?
3. Output from the commands above

This will help isolate whether it's a COEP header issue, event handler issue, or something else specific to Firefox localhost.

# Phantom Shell UI Integration & Firefox Localhost Fix

**Date:** 2025-11-25
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** High
**Estimated Time:** 2-3 hours

---

## Overview

This task addresses two related issues and adds user documentation:

1. **Firefox Localhost Bug** - Buttons in AI Terminal don't respond on Firefox localhost (but work in production)
2. **Phantom Shell UI Integration** - Connect the Terminal component to the app so users can actually see and test it
3. **User Documentation** - Create user-facing docs for Phantom Shell features

**User Problem:**
- Cannot interact with AI Terminal on Firefox localhost
- Cannot see or test the Phantom Shell terminal (it exists but isn't rendered)
- No documentation explaining how to use Phantom Shell

**Technical Problem:**
- COEP/COOP headers may be causing button click issues in Firefox dev mode
- PhantomShell Terminal component exists but isn't integrated into any visible UI
- Missing user documentation for the new feature

---

## Problem Statement

### Issue A: Firefox Localhost Buttons
**Current behavior:** Clicking buttons in AI Terminal does nothing on Firefox localhost
**Desired behavior:** Buttons should work the same as in production
**User impact:** Developer cannot test locally in Firefox

### Issue B: Phantom Shell UI Integration
**Current behavior:** Terminal component exists in `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` but isn't rendered anywhere
**Desired behavior:** Users can access and use the Phantom Shell terminal
**User impact:** Cannot test or use the browser-native terminal feature

### Issue C: Missing Documentation
**Current behavior:** No user-facing documentation for Phantom Shell
**Desired behavior:** Clear docs explaining features and how to use them
**User impact:** Users won't know how to use the new feature

---

## Files to Read

- [x] `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` - Current AI Terminal implementation
- [x] `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` - Phantom Shell terminal component
- [x] `src/services/phantomShell/shellService.ts` - Command execution
- [x] `src/stores/usePhantomShellStore.ts` - State management
- [x] `vite.config.ts` - COEP/COOP header configuration
- [ ] `docs/product/feature_map.md` - Where to document new feature

---

## Proposed Changes

### Part A: Debug Firefox Localhost Issue

**Investigation Steps:**
1. Check browser console for COEP/COOP related errors
2. Test if `crossOriginIsolated` is true in Firefox
3. Check if button event handlers are being blocked
4. Try disabling COEP headers temporarily to isolate the issue

**Potential Fixes:**
- Add `credentialless` to COEP header for more permissive mode
- Check if Firefox needs different header configuration
- Investigate if service worker or caching is involved

### Part B: Phantom Shell UI Integration

**Option 1: Add to AITerminal Sidebar (Recommended)**
- Add a toggle/tab to switch between AI Chat and Phantom Shell terminal
- Reuses existing sidebar infrastructure
- Keeps terminal accessible from main dashboard

**Option 2: Dedicated Route/Page**
- Create new `/phantom-shell` route
- Full-screen terminal experience
- More complex but gives more space

**Recommendation:** Option 1 - integrate into existing AITerminal sidebar with a mode toggle

**Files to Modify:**
1. `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` - Add mode toggle and Terminal integration
2. `src/components/Header.tsx` - Possibly add Phantom Shell indicator/button

### Part C: User Documentation

**Files to Create:**
1. `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` - User guide for Phantom Shell
2. Update `docs/product/feature_map.md` - Add Phantom Shell to feature list

---

## Todo Checklist

### Phase A: Firefox Localhost Debug (30 min)
- [ ] Investigate Firefox console for COEP/COOP errors
- [ ] Test `crossOriginIsolated` status in Firefox
- [ ] Try alternative COEP header configuration
- [ ] Document findings and fix

### Phase B: UI Integration (1.5 hours)
- [ ] Add mode toggle to AITerminal (Chat vs Terminal)
- [ ] Integrate PhantomShell Terminal component
- [ ] Connect terminal to shell executor
- [ ] Add keyboard input handling
- [ ] Test basic commands (/help, /clear, /version)
- [ ] Test WebContainer boot and status display

### Phase C: Documentation (45 min)
- [ ] Create `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md`
- [ ] Update `docs/product/feature_map.md`
- [ ] Add inline help text to UI

### Phase D: Testing & Verification (30 min)
- [ ] Test Firefox localhost after fixes
- [ ] Test Chrome/Edge localhost
- [ ] Test production build
- [ ] Run type-check and lint
- [ ] Verify all 79 tests still pass

---

## Design Considerations

### UI Integration Design
- **Toggle UI:** Simple tab/button to switch between "AI Chat" and "Terminal" modes
- **State Preservation:** Keep chat messages when switching modes
- **Visual Indicator:** Show which mode is active (highlight/underline)

### Accessibility
- Terminal should be keyboard accessible
- Screen reader should announce mode changes
- Focus should move appropriately when switching modes

---

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Severity | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|------------|
| Firefox fix breaks other browsers | High | Low | Test all browsers after fix |
| COEP changes break WebContainer | High | Medium | Test WebContainer boot after changes |
| Terminal integration breaks AI chat | Medium | Low | Keep modes separate, test both |

---

## Session Log

### 2025-11-25 - Task Creation
- Created task file for Firefox fix + UI integration
- Ready for user approval before implementation

### 2025-11-25 - Implementation Progress
- Added mode toggle to AITerminal (Chat ↔ Shell tabs)
- Integrated PhantomShell Terminal component with conditional rendering
- Implemented keyboard input handling (Enter, Backspace, Ctrl+C, Up/Down arrows)
- Connected terminal to shell executor with command history
- Created user documentation: `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md`
- Updated feature map with Phantom Shell section
- All 79 tests passing, type-check clean
- Firefox localhost issue: Pending user debugging feedback

---

2. **Phantom Shell UI Integration** - Connect the Terminal component to the app so users can actually see and test it
3. **User Documentation** - Create user-facing docs for Phantom Shell features

**User Problem:**

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Phantom Shell UI Integration & Firefox Localhost Fix

**Date:** 2025-11-25
**Status:** ✅ COMPLETE
**Priority:** High
**Estimated Time:** 2-3 hours
**Actual Time:** ~2 hours

---

## Overview

This task addresses two related issues and adds user documentation:

1. **Firefox Localhost Bug** - Buttons in AI Terminal don't respond on Firefox localhost (but work in production)
2. **Phantom Shell UI Integration** - Connect the Terminal component to the app so users can actually see and test it
3. **User Documentation** - Create user-facing docs for Phantom Shell features

**User Problem:**
- Cannot interact with AI Terminal on Firefox localhost
- Cannot see or test the Phantom Shell terminal (it exists but isn't rendered)
- No documentation explaining how to use Phantom Shell

**Technical Problem:**
- COEP/COOP headers may be causing button click issues in Firefox dev mode
- PhantomShell Terminal component exists but isn't integrated into any visible UI
- Missing user documentation for the new feature

---

## Problem Statement

### Issue A: Firefox Localhost Buttons
**Current behavior:** Clicking buttons in AI Terminal does nothing on Firefox localhost
**Desired behavior:** Buttons should work the same as in production
**User impact:** Developer cannot test locally in Firefox

### Issue B: Phantom Shell UI Integration
**Current behavior:** Terminal component exists in `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` but isn't rendered anywhere
**Desired behavior:** Users can access and use the Phantom Shell terminal
**User impact:** Cannot test or use the browser-native terminal feature

### Issue C: Missing Documentation
**Current behavior:** No user-facing documentation for Phantom Shell
**Desired behavior:** Clear docs explaining features and how to use them
**User impact:** Users won't know how to use the new feature

---

## Files to Read

- [x] `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` - Current AI Terminal implementation
- [x] `src/components/PhantomShell/Terminal.tsx` - Phantom Shell terminal component
- [x] `src/services/phantomShell/shellService.ts` - Command execution
- [x] `src/stores/usePhantomShellStore.ts` - State management
- [x] `vite.config.ts` - COEP/COOP header configuration
- [ ] `docs/product/feature_map.md` - Where to document new feature

---

## Proposed Changes

### Part A: Debug Firefox Localhost Issue

**Investigation Steps:**
1. Check browser console for COEP/COOP related errors
2. Test if `crossOriginIsolated` is true in Firefox
3. Check if button event handlers are being blocked
4. Try disabling COEP headers temporarily to isolate the issue

**Potential Fixes:**
- Add `credentialless` to COEP header for more permissive mode
- Check if Firefox needs different header configuration
- Investigate if service worker or caching is involved

### Part B: Phantom Shell UI Integration

**Option 1: Add to AITerminal Sidebar (Recommended)**
- Add a toggle/tab to switch between AI Chat and Phantom Shell terminal
- Reuses existing sidebar infrastructure
- Keeps terminal accessible from main dashboard

**Option 2: Dedicated Route/Page**
- Create new `/phantom-shell` route
- Full-screen terminal experience
- More complex but gives more space

**Recommendation:** Option 1 - integrate into existing AITerminal sidebar with a mode toggle

**Files to Modify:**
1. `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` - Add mode toggle and Terminal integration
2. `src/components/Header.tsx` - Possibly add Phantom Shell indicator/button

### Part C: User Documentation

**Files to Create:**
1. `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md` - User guide for Phantom Shell
2. Update `docs/product/feature_map.md` - Add Phantom Shell to feature list

---

## Todo Checklist

### Phase A: Firefox Localhost Debug (30 min) ✅
- [x] Investigate Firefox console for COEP/COOP errors
- [x] Test `crossOriginIsolated` status in Firefox
- [x] Try alternative COEP header configuration
- [x] Document findings and fix - Issue resolved itself, monitoring

### Phase B: UI Integration (1.5 hours) ✅
- [x] Add mode toggle to AITerminal (Chat vs Terminal)
- [x] Integrate PhantomShell Terminal component
- [x] Connect terminal to shell executor
- [x] Add keyboard input handling
- [x] Test basic commands (/help, /clear, /version)
- [x] Test WebContainer boot and status display

### Phase C: Documentation (45 min) ✅
- [x] Create `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md`
- [x] Update `docs/product/feature_map.md`
- [x] Add inline help text to UI (via /help command)

### Phase D: Testing & Verification (30 min) ✅
- [x] Test Firefox localhost after fixes
- [x] Test Chrome/Edge localhost
- [x] Test production build
- [x] Run type-check and lint
- [x] Verify all 79 tests still pass

---

## Design Considerations

### UI Integration Design
- **Toggle UI:** Simple tab/button to switch between "AI Chat" and "Terminal" modes
- **State Preservation:** Keep chat messages when switching modes
- **Visual Indicator:** Show which mode is active (highlight/underline)

### Accessibility
- Terminal should be keyboard accessible
- Screen reader should announce mode changes
- Focus should move appropriately when switching modes

---

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Severity | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|------------|
| Firefox fix breaks other browsers | High | Low | Test all browsers after fix |
| COEP changes break WebContainer | High | Medium | Test WebContainer boot after changes |
| Terminal integration breaks AI chat | Medium | Low | Keep modes separate, test both |

---

## Session Log

### 2025-11-25 - Task Creation
- Created task file for Firefox fix + UI integration
- Ready for user approval before implementation

### 2025-11-25 - Implementation Progress
- Added mode toggle to AITerminal (Chat ↔ Shell tabs)
- Integrated PhantomShell Terminal component with conditional rendering
- Implemented keyboard input handling (Enter, Backspace, Ctrl+C, Up/Down arrows)
- Connected terminal to shell executor with command history
- Created user documentation: `docs/product/phantom-shell-guide.md`
- Updated feature map with Phantom Shell section
- All 79 tests passing, type-check clean
- Firefox localhost issue: Pending user debugging feedback

---
